quotes.verbatim_quote:
The augmented scale, like the diminished scale, is a symmetrical scale. This time the intervals al-
ternate between half-sieps and one and one-half steps (minor thirds or augmented seconds). On C
the scale would be spelled C, D-sharp, E, G, A-flat, B, C. Note that there are, because of the wide
augmented second intervals, only six tones in the scale.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This is the foundational definition of the augmented scale, giving its interval structure, spelling, and the reason it has only six tones — the core theoretical anchor for everything that follows.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Only four (chro-
matically adjacent) augmented scales exist.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This rule establishes the symmetry property that limits the augmented scale to four distinct transpositions, a critical fact for knowing which scales to practice and how they overlap.

quotes.topic: Augmented scale derived from augmented triads
quotes.verbatim_quote:
As its name implies. the augmented scale is derived from the use of augmented triads. If we examine
the above spelling of the C augmented scale, we see that it contains cwo augmented triads, chromati-
cally adjacent: B, D-sharp, G (or F double sharp) and C, E, A-flat (or G-sharp).
quotes.why_load_bearing: Explains the harmonic origin of the scale by showing it contains two chromatically adjacent augmented triads, giving the student both a mnemonic and a chord-scale relationship to exploit.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
In addition to fitting augmented triads, the augmented scale fits a rarely-used chord, the major
seventh chord with an augmented fifth (M7+5)°", Again, since this chord is relatively new, like the
scale, it could enjoy wider use in the future, because both the chord and the scale present interesting
sounds that might prove attractive to the jazz improviser. With slightly less effect, the scale can be
applied to the major seventh chord (M7).
quotes.why_load_bearing: Provides the definitive chord-scale mapping — M7+5 as the primary target chord and M7 as a secondary option — which tells the improviser exactly when to deploy this scale.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
It also carries much potential for becoming a “free form”
device, because of its mystical, keyless sound and its symmetry in construction.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Extends the scale's application beyond specific chord contexts to free/atonal settings, broadening the student's conceptual framework for when and why to use it.
